# `ocx_python` fixture corpus

Generated by [`generate.py`](./generate.py) — deterministic, no external
dependencies (stdlib only: `zipfile`, `hashlib`, `tomllib` for validation).
Re-run after editing the generator:

```sh
python3 crates/ocx_python/tests/fixtures/generate.py
```

Determinism is load-bearing: two runs must produce byte-identical output
(fixed DOS-epoch zip mtimes, `ZIP_STORED`, sorted entries, fixed unix
mode/owner per entry). Verified via `diff -r` + `sha256sum` across two
independent output directories — see the generator's module docstring.

Wheel `url` fields in every `pylock/*.toml` are templated as
`{ASSET_BASE}/<filename>` (Decision D6): the acceptance harness substitutes
its local `asset_server` base URL at test time so lock fixtures stay
hermetic. `valid-multi.toml`'s wheel `hashes.sha256` values are the real
sha256 of the checked-in wheel bytes in `wheels/` — cross-checked at
generation time, so an end-to-end test can serve these same files and have
hash verification pass.

## Wheels (`wheels/*.whl`)

| Filename | Exercises |
|---|---|
| `purelib_pkg-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl` | Pure-python wheel, single tag |
| `cext_pkg-2.0.0-cp312-cp312-linux_x86_64.whl` | cpXY C-extension stub (`platlib`, `Root-Is-Purelib: false`) |
| `abi3_pkg-3.1.0-cp312-abi3-manylinux_2_28_x86_64.whl` | `abi3` stable-ABI tag spanning CPython minors |
| `google_cloud_foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl` + `google_cloud_bar-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl` | PEP 420 implicit namespace pair — both under `google/cloud/`, distinct leaves, no `__init__.py` in the shared namespace dirs; proves namespace-dir merge is not a `collide` false positive |
| `data_pkg-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl` | `.data/scripts` (executable launcher, mode `0o755`) + `.data/data` (`share/…` relocation target) |
| `console_pkg-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl` | `[console_scripts]`: a plain script, an extras-gated script (`blackd = blackd:main [d]`), and a dotted-attribute-chain reference (`foo = console_pkg.mod:Class.method`) |
| `legacy_ns_pkg-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl` | Legacy pre-PEP-420 namespace declaration via top-level `legacy_ns_pkg-nspkg.pth` (setuptools `declare_namespace` boilerplate) |
| `universal_pkg-1.0.0-py2.py3-none-any.whl` | Compressed multi-tag filename; `WHEEL` explodes it into two `Tag:` lines (`py2-none-any`, `py3-none-any`) |

## Lock fixtures (`pylock/*.toml`)

| File | Exercises |
|---|---|
| `valid-multi.toml` | All 9 wheels above, one package each, real hashes — the happy-path multi-package lock |
| `sdist-only.toml` | A package with `[packages.sdist]` and no `wheels` key at all → `LockError::SdistOnly` |
| `missing-hash.toml` | A wheel table with `url` but no `hashes` key → `LockError::MissingHash` |
| `marker-forks.toml` | OS fork (`colorama; sys_platform == "win32"`) and inverted OS fork (`watchdog; platform_system != "Darwin"`) |
| `environments.toml` | Top-level `environments` key scoping the lock to a single target environment |

## Regenerating

`generate.py` also runs a self-check per wheel (`self_check`): valid zip,
dist-info trio present, sorted entries. It has no test-framework dependency —
the determinism guarantee is proven externally by running it twice into
different output directories and diffing (see Gate below), not by a unit
test *of* the generator.

**Gate**: `python3 generate.py --out /tmp/a && python3 generate.py --out /tmp/b && diff -r /tmp/a /tmp/b`
must report no differences.
